title: KeyRevokeBody v0.1
description: Cycle 1 substrate-core schema migration 001 baseline for the key-revoke channel kind per Pair B slice plan body §2.5 + §4.3 LOCKED. Posted by the operator revoking their own Ed25519 key OR by cohort members co-signing a revocation when a compromise is detected. The wire-format here drives <nato>.history.json maintenance which feeds resolveKeyAtTime (key-surface.ts) which feeds verifyChannelAuditChain (verify.ts): revoked entries map to breaks[].reason = 'revoked-key' per the 3-class break taxonomy (sub-Obs-6a + DC-5) distinct from 'tamper' (signature failure) and 'key-rotation-discontinuity' (chain gap / no covering history entry). See src/channels/key-revoke.ts KeyRevokeBody type + parseKeyRevokeBody function for parser implementation. Pair B Delta-pen capacity-take per cohort feedback-cohort-standby-standoff-anti-pattern rule + Pair B §5 flexibility-clause invocation at Charlie 19:42Z tool-flow-accuracy explicit-defer.

kind_version.description: Schema version literal (stays at 1; future migrations are additive optional).

revoked_nato.description: NATO identifier of the operator whose key is being revoked (e.g., 'Alpha', 'Delta'). Non-empty-post-trim.

revoked_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the revoked public key (hex; matches KeyHistoryEntry.fingerprint per PR-A3 key-surface.ts). Used by verifier-side history-file maintenance to mark the specific key entry as status='revoked'.

revoked_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the key was revoked. Verifier uses this to mark KeyHistoryEntry.active_until (transitions the entry from status='active' to status='revoked').

reason.description: 3-class revocation reason per §2.5 LOCKED + sub-Obs-6a — distinct operational remediation paths. compromise = private key suspected leaked/exfiltrated; rotation = operator-intentional key rotation (no compromise); operator-departure = NATO identity retiring from cohort.

replacement_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the replacement key when a new active key is taking over (rotation scenario). null when no replacement exists (compromise without rotation, operator-departure, emergency revocation). Must be explicit (present in body; not undefined).

signed_by.description: NATO identifiers of cohort members signing this revocation. Min-1 (revoking operator themselves). For compromise scenarios, future cohort design MAY require N-of-cohort co-signing (deferred per §2.5 prose; schema enforces ≥1 only).

x-claude-conductor.supersedes_migration_note: v0.1 baseline for key-revoke kind; no prior schema. Future migrations may add fields (e.g., DSSE-wrapping for non-repudiation per future PR; multi-signer N-of-cohort co-signing per §2.5 compromise-scenario clause).
x-claude-conductor.next_migration: 002 (TBD; future cycle may add DSSE-wrapping + N-of-cohort co-sig requirements when compromise scenarios empirically surface)
x-claude-conductor.ssot_pen: Pair B Delta (capacity-take per Pair B §5 flexibility-clause; Charlie tool-flow-accuracy explicit-defer at 19:42Z)
x-claude-conductor.audit_shadow_pen: Pair B Charlie (inverted micro-split; audit-shadow at Charlie next-resume)
x-claude-conductor.cross_pair_contract: plans/cycle-1-substrate-core-slice-plan-2026-05-26.md §4.3 (key-rotation contract; verifier-side resolveKeyAtTime maps revoked entries to revoked-key break reason)
x-claude-conductor.cohort_ratify_audit_trail_note: Ratify body_refs + session UUIDs + channel id documented in PR commit message body + cohort channel (not in schema file to keep schema operator-portable + free of bernstein arc-specific anonymization-leak class identifiers per check-generic-paths CGP-001/CGP-004 lessons from PR-A1).
